title: PV Sindhu Answers Doubters with Emotional Japan Open Crown
indian_angle: India's top female shuttler PV Sindhu won a major international title, marking a significant return to form.
summary: PV Sindhu secured the Japan Open title, emotionally reflecting on her comeback and silencing critics who questioned her career.

title: PV Sindhu, Ayush Shetty Exit China Open After Wasting Match Points
indian_angle: Two prominent Indian shuttlers faced early exits from a prestigious Super 1000 tournament, highlighting challenges at the elite level.
summary: PV Sindhu and Ayush Shetty both exited the China Open Super 1000, with Sindhu notably losing to Chen Yufei after holding four match points.

title: Varshini-Nayana Win Chennai Senior Ranking Title, Target World Top 80
indian_angle: This young Indian women's doubles pair secured a significant domestic title and are now setting sights on improving their international ranking.
summary: V.S. Varshini and Nayana S. Oasis celebrated their first women's doubles title at the Yonex-Sunrise All India Senior Ranking tournament in Chennai, expressing hopes to break into the world's top 80.

title: India to Host BWF World Championships 2026 in Delhi
indian_angle: India is set to host the prestigious BWF World Championships in Delhi, a major event for the sport in the country.
summary: The Badminton Association of India has issued an Expression of Interest for the BWF World Championships 2026, with Delhi confirmed as the host city, promising a significant event for Indian badminton.

title: New 3x15 Scoring System Implemented for Junior Domestic Tournaments
indian_angle: A significant change in scoring format has been introduced for junior domestic tournaments, impacting player development and match dynamics across India.
summary: The Badminton Association of India has implemented a new 3x15 scoring system for all domestic ranking tournaments and national championships in the U-13, U-15, and U-17 age groups.

title: Player Eligibility for World Junior Championships 2026 Announced
indian_angle: The selection criteria for Indian junior players for the upcoming BWF World Junior Championships in Cairo, Egypt, have been released, guiding aspiring talents.
summary: BAI has released the eligibility criteria for Indian players aiming to participate in the BWF World Junior Championships 2026 in Cairo, Egypt, along with details on selection tournaments.
